advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Validate/resolve the id in `use_identity` (resolve alias/short forms and confirm it is an existing identity ticket) before writing the pointer, or emit an error on a non-identity/nonexistent id.
evidence: src/rebar/_commands/identity.py:183 `def use_identity(identity_id: str, *, repo_root=None) -> None:` — body just calls `_write_pointer(identity_id, ...)` with no lookup/resolution
src/rebar/_commands/identity.py:288 `if verb == "use": ... use_identity(rest[0], repo_root=repo_root)` — passes CLI arg verbatim
src/rebar/_commands/identity.py:84 `d = os.path.join(tracker, ticket_id)` in `_is_identity` — exact dir-name match, no alias/prefix resolution, so an aliased pointer never resolves
The `use` verb accepts `rest[0]` without checking `_is_identity`, unlike `resolve` which validates via `_is_identity`.
advisory.finding: `rebar identity use <id>` (and `use_identity`) writes the raw argument to the pointer file with no validation that it names an existing identity ticket, and does not resolve alias/short-id/prefix forms. Because the pointer read path (`_is_identity`) uses `os.path.join(tracker, ticket_id)` against exact canonical directory names, a user passing an alias or a typo'd/nonexistent id silently writes a dangling pointer that `resolve_current_identity` can never match (it falls through to git-email match instead), with no error surfaced.
scenarios: `rebar identity use semifluid-clotty-plover` (an alias) writes a pointer that resolve never matches
`rebar identity use <typo-id>` silently succeeds and prints 'Now using identity <typo>'

advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Enforce non-empty `email` (and `name`) in `create_identity_core` so the CLI, library, and MCP surfaces validate identically.
evidence: src/rebar/_commands/identity.py:157 `def create_identity_core(name: str, email: str, ...)` — no non-empty check on name/email; delegates to create_core which only validates title (name), not email
src/rebar/_commands/composer.py:184 `if identity:` then copies email/mappings/keys — no email validation
src/rebar/_commands/identity.py:252 `if not email: raise CommandError(...)` — the non-empty check exists ONLY in the CLI parser, not the core/library/MCP path
advisory.finding: Library `create_identity` and the MCP `create_identity` tool accept an empty `email` (and empty mappings/keys), whereas the CLI's `_parse_create` requires `--email` non-empty. An identity created programmatically with `email=""` can never be matched by `resolve_current_identity` (git email is never empty at match time) yet is otherwise valid, and validation now diverges across the CLI vs library/MCP surfaces.

advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a test for `rebar identity create --self` asserting `.rebar/current_identity` points at the newly created id.
evidence: ABSENCE: `search_files` for `--self` and `use_self` under tests/ returned no matches
test_cli_identity_create_and_show_e2e exercises `rebar identity create` but omits --self and never asserts the pointer contents
advisory.finding: The CLI `identity create --self` flag (use_self), which writes the .rebar/current_identity pointer as a create-time side effect, has no test. A regression that drops or ignores --self (pointer never published) would pass all existing tests.
scenarios: A change to _parse_create or identity_cli silently stops honoring --self; resolve_current_identity then never finds the just-created identity.
advisory.suggested_fix: Extend test_cli_identity_create_and_show_e2e (or add a sibling) to run create with --self and assert json.loads((store/'.rebar'/'current_identity').read_text())['identity_id'] == ident_id.

advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a test asserting an `identity` ticket cannot be transitioned/closed (transition_core refuses) and that the close gate short-circuits.
evidence: transition_close.py:96-100: `# session_log / code_review are lifecycle-exempt — they cannot be transitioned... if ticket_type in ("session_log", "code_review", "identity"): return None`

ABSENCE: `search_files` for `transition` in tests/unit/test_identity_ddbe_heldout.py returned no matches; the held-out oracle covers list/ready/next_batch/gates but not lifecycle transition/close of an identity
advisory.finding: The transition_close diff adds `identity` to the lifecycle-exempt short-circuit (claiming an identity cannot be transitioned/closed), but no test asserts an identity ticket actually refuses transition/close. The new close-gate exemption behavior is untested.
scenarios: The lifecycle exemption regresses so an identity is treated as transitionable/closable (or the doomed close fires the billable verifier); no identity test catches it.
advisory.suggested_fix: Add a held-out test that attempts rebar.transition(ident, ...) / close on an identity and asserts it is refused (CommandError / non-zero exit), mirroring the session_log lifecycle-exemption test.

coaching.coaching: Add a regression test covering the CLI --self flag's pointer-write side effect.
coaching.subject: the CLI --self flag's pointer-write side effect

coaching.coaching: Add a regression test covering identity ticket's refusal to transition/close.
coaching.subject: identity ticket's refusal to transition/close
